hypothesis: If machine learning interatomic potentials (EquiformerV2 and eSEN) pretrained on large materials datasets are used to generate converged initial pathway guesses for solid-state nudged elastic band calculations, then the subsequent DFT-based SSNEB refinement will require significantly fewer iterations to reach convergence compared to pure DFT-SSNEB, thereby reducing total computational cost while maintaining DFT-level accuracy in identifying metastable states and transition pathways.
